description_long: This is a tall, slender stone obelisk with a square base, rising vertically and tapering to a pointed tip. The surface is weathered and light gray, with visible signs of aging and some moss or plant growth at the base. The lower section features engraved inscriptions on multiple faces, set into a slightly protruding plinth. The obelisk stands on a multi-tiered pedestal, which is also made of stone and has a simple, robust design. The overall appearance is solemn and commemorative, typical of monuments found in parks, cemeteries, or public squares. The structure is solid and immovable, designed to serve as a lasting marker or memorial.
description: A tall, weathered stone obelisk with engraved inscriptions on a square base, featuring moss at the bottom and a pointed tip.
description_view_0: Front view showing clear inscriptions on the base and some moss at the lower right corner.
description_view_1: Side view with visible inscriptions and more moss along the left edge of the base.
description_view_2: Rear view with no visible inscriptions, showing a plain stone surface and some plant growth at the base.
description_view_3: Oblique rear view with partial inscriptions visible on the right and significant moss on the left side of the base.
attribute_inscription: Engraved inscriptions are present on the lower section of the obelisk's base, visible on at least two faces. The text appears to be in English, with a serif style, but is partially weathered and not fully legible from the images.
attribute_base_design: The base is square and multi-tiered, constructed from the same stone as the obelisk. It features a slightly protruding plinth with engraved inscriptions and a simple, robust design. Moss and plant growth are visible around the bottom edges.
attribute_tip_design: The tip is sharply pointed and smoothly finished, with no visible ornamentation or unique features.
attribute_surrounding_features: Sparse patches of moss and small plants are present at the base, with no other notable surrounding features visible.

license_info.creator_display_name: Santa Cruz Museum of Art and History
